name: Robert Peters
bio: Robert Peters is listed as a Democratic candidate for Illinois' 2nd Congressional District in 2026. He serves in the Illinois Senate and entered the open-seat primary after Robin Kelly pursued a Senate bid. (Source: Shaw Local candidate list, March 5, 2026: https://www.shawlocal.com/northwest-herald/2026/03/05/illinois-primary-ballot-heres-whos-running-in-the-march-17-election/)

jacket.note: ~$1.13M raised as of Feb 25, 2026 (FEC). FEC ID: H6IL02298
jacket.donors_note: No itemized donor breakdown available yet. total_raised reflects FEC aggregate total (H6IL02298) — detailed donor records pending ETL import.

key_votes.summary: Illinois Safety, Accountability, Fairness and Equity-Today Act — eliminated cash bail, expanded pretrial rights, strengthened accountability for law enforcement. Peters was the lead Senate sponsor.
Tenant protections and just-cause eviction standards. Peters was a primary Senate sponsor.

career_history.highlight: Progressive leader on criminal justice and housing
Tenant rights, housing justice, and anti-violence work

career_history.org: Legal Aid Chicago / Prairie State Legal Services

policy_platform.position: Led passage of SAFE-T Act in Illinois Senate; consistent advocate for ending cash bail and reducing pretrial incarceration.
Supports strong tenant protections, just-cause eviction standards, and affordable housing funding.
Supports gun control legislation; advocates for community investment as violence prevention.

social_pulse.summary: Positive among progressive activists; well-regarded as policy leader on criminal justice in Illinois. Moderate public profile statewide.

trust_indicators.label: SAFE-T Act author — led bail reform in Illinois
Former legal aid attorney — public interest background
